title: Miconia neourceolata
intro: Miconia neourceolata, also known as the 'Cup of the Forest' or 'Forest Cup', is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders and infections. The bioactive constituents of Miconia neourceolata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Miconia neourceolata is often used in traditional medicine and is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, as well as being used in the production of herbal capsules and tinctures.
taxonomy: Miconia neourceolata is a species within the family Melastomataceae. This family, commonly known as the melastome family, is a group of flowering plants. The family Melastomataceae is part of the larger clade of rosids in the angiosperm classification. The clade of rosids is a grouping within the broader category of flowering plants. The classification of Miconia neourceolata reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plant species.
